diffs.diff:
--- a/explore_data.py
+++ b/explore_data.py
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+# Explore the data structure

+import numpy as np

+import json

+

+data_dir = '/data'

+

+# Load training data

+train_data = np.load(f'{data_dir}/train_data.npy')

+val_data = np.load(f'{data_dir}/val_data.npy')

+norm_stats = json.load(open(f'{data_dir}/norm_stats.json'))

+lat = np.load(f'{data_dir}/lat.npy')

+lon = np.load(f'{data_dir}/lon.npy')

+

+print('Train data shape:', train_data.shape)

+print('Val data shape:', val_data.shape)

+print('Normalization stats:', norm_stats)

+print('Lat shape:', lat.shape)

+print('Lon shape:', lon.shape)

+print('Sample train data stats - Z500:', train_data[:, 0].mean(), train_data[:, 0].std())

+print('Sample train data stats - T850:', train_data[:, 1].mean(), train_data[:, 1].std())

+

+# Show how to create input-target pairs

+seq_len = 3  # Input sequence length

+pred_offset = 12  # Predict 72 hours ahead (12 * 6 hours)

+num_samples = train_data.shape[0]

+

+print(f'\nFor creating training pairs:')

+print(f'Input shape would be: [{seq_len}, 2, 32, 64]')

+print(f'Target shape would be: [2, 32, 64]')

+print(f'Max possible samples: {num_samples - seq_len - pred_offset + 1}')
